entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop, Arachis hypogaea L., susceptible to late leaf spot and rust.

entities.name: LATE LEAF SPOT
entities.description: A foliar disease affecting groundnut, causing yield loss.

entities.name: RUST
entities.description: A foliar disease affecting groundnut, causing yield loss.

entities.name: YIELD LOSS
entities.description: Reduction in crop production, quantified as 50-70% in this context.

entities.name: TAG 24
entities.description: A parental line used in the mapping population.

entities.name: GPBD 4
entities.description: A parental line used in the mapping population.

entities.name: RECOMBINANT INBRED LINES
entities.description: A population derived from crossing two parental lines, used for QTL analysis.

entities.name: QTL MAPPING
entities.description: A statistical method used to identify genomic regions associated with traits.

entities.name: PHENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of observing and recording characteristics of an organism.

entities.name: ARTIFICIAL DISEASE EPIDEMICS
entities.description: Controlled conditions used to induce disease development for phenotyping.

entities.name: HERITABILITY
entities.description: The proportion of phenotypic variation attributable to genetic factors.

entities.name: SSR MARKERS
entities.description: Simple sequence repeat markers used for genotyping.

entities.name: LINKAGE MAP
entities.description: A genetic map showing the relative positions of markers.

entities.name: QTLS
entities.description: Quantitative trait loci, genomic regions associated with traits.

entities.name: QTLRUST01
entities.description: A major QTL associated with rust resistance.

entities.name: IPAHM 103
entities.description: An SSR marker linked to the QTLrust01 QTL.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding technique using markers to introgress desired genes.

entities.name: TG 26
entities.description: A parental line used in another mapping population.
